title: Heartburn and acid reflux during pregnancy
excerpt: Why reflux worsens in pregnancy, meal timing tricks, soothing foods, and when to discuss medication with your clinician.
content:
## Why heartburn is common

Progesterone relaxes the esophageal sphincter; the growing uterus presses upward. Symptoms often peak in the third trimester.

## Eating tips

- Smaller meals; finish dinner 2–3 hours before lying down.
- Mild flavors; reduce very spicy or fatty triggers if they worsen symptoms.
- Elevate head of bed slightly.

## Soothing foods

Oat porridge, banana, yogurt, ginger tea in moderation if tolerated.

## When to see your doctor

Pain with swallowing, vomiting blood, or symptoms unresponsive to safe lifestyle changes — ask about approved antacids.
